LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANT BARCLAYS BANK DELAWARE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /ADJUDICATION - DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS TIMELY FILED AND PROPERLY MADE, NOTWITHSTANDING THAT DEFENDANT FILED A DEMURRER EARLIER IN THE CASE. FURTHER, DEFENDANT IS NOT ESTOPPED FROM MAKING THE MOTION OR THE ASSERTING THE ARGUMENTS IN ITS. THE COURT CONSIDERS PLAINTIFF'S LATE SUBMITTED AMENDED SEPARATE STATEMENT FILED DECEMBER 16, 2013, ALTHOUGH PLAINTIFF DID NOT SEEK LEAVE TO FILE LATE ADDITIONAL PAPERS. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE ARE OVERRULED IN THEIR ENTIRETY. AS TO THE BREACH OF CONTRACT CAUSE OF ACTION, DEFENDANT MET ITS BURDEN IN PROVIDING EVIDENCE THAT SHOWS THAT PLAINTIFF CANNOT ESTABLISH EACH ELEMENT OF THIS CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF CANNOT ESTABLISH THAT HE PERFORMED UNDER THE CARDMEMBER AGREEMENT (THE "AGREEMENT") BECAUSE THE AGREEMENT REQUIRED MONTHLY PAYMENTS IF THERE WAS A BALANCE ON THE CARD. (DECLARATION OF PERI HUTT, EX. A; DEFENDANT'S UNDISPUTED FACT ("UF") NO. 5.) IT IS UNDISPUTED THAT PLAINTIFF STOPPED MAKING THESE PAYMENTS. (UF NO. 21.) PLAINTIFF CANNOT ESTABLISH THAT THE AGREEMENT WAS BREACHED BECAUSE THE EVIDENCE REVEALS THERE WAS NO DISPUTE TO BE RESOLVED. PLAINTIFF AGREED TO A 17.99% INTEREST RATE, PURSUANT TO THE UNAMBIGUOUS TERMS OF THE AGREEMENT. (HUTT DEC., EX. A.) AS TO THE DECLARATORY RELIEF CAUSE OF ACTION, DEFENDANT HAS MET ITS BURDEN IN PROVIDING EVIDENCE THAT AN ACCORD AND SATISFACTION DID NOT OCCUR. THE UNAMBIGUOUS LANGUAGE OF THE AGREEMENT STATES THAT DEFENDANT "CAN ACCEPT LATE PAYMENTS, PARTIAL PAYMENTS, CHECKS AND MONEY ORDERS MARKED 'PAID IN FULL' OR LANGUAGE HAVING THAT SAME EFFECT WITHOUT LOSING ANY OF [ITS] RIGHTS UNDER THIS AGREEMENT." (HUTT DEC., EX. A; UF NO. 5.) FURTHER, THE UNDISPUTED EVIDENCE ESTABLISHES THAT THE AMOUNT OF THE CLAIM WAS NOT SUBJECT TO A BONA FIDE DISPUTE, AS REQUIRED BY CAL. COM. CODE SEC. 3311. (FIRST AMEND

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication
Off Calendar Dec-27-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. JAMES A. MICHEL, TEL. NO. 239-4949 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. RAYMOND Y. KIM OF REED SMITH LLP, TEL. NO. (213) 457-8105 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT BARCLAYS BANK DELAWARE's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication - Defendant's Motion for Summary Judgment is granted. The motion for summary judgment is timely filed and properly made, notwithstanding that Defendant filed a demurrer earlier in the case. Further, Defendant is not estopped from making the motion or the asserting the arguments in its. The Court considers Plaintiff's late submitted Amended Separate Statement filed December 16, 2013, although Plaintiff did not seek leave to file late additional papers. Plaintiff's objections to evidence are overruled in their entirety. As to the breach of contract cause of action, Defendant met its burden in providing evidence that shows that Plaintiff cannot establish each element of this cause of action. Plaintiff cannot establish that he performed under the Cardmember Agreement (the "Agreement") because the Agreement required monthly payments if there was a balance on the card. (Declaration of Peri Hutt, Ex. A; Defendant's Undisputed Fact ("UF") no. 5.) It is undisputed that Plaintiff stopped making these payments. (UF no. 21.) Plaintiff cannot establish that the agreement was breached because the evidence reveals there was no dispute to be resolved. Plaintiff agreed to a 17.99% interest rate, pursuant to the unambiguous terms of the Agreement. (Hutt Dec., Ex. A.) As to the declaratory relief cause of action, Defendant has met its burden in
providing evidence that an accord and satisfaction did not occur. The unambiguous language of the Agreement states that Defendant "can accept late payments, partial payments, checks and money orders marked 'Paid in Full' or language having that same effect without losing any of [its] rights under this Agreement." (Hutt Dec., Ex. A; UF no. 5.) Further, the undisputed evidence establishes that the amount of the claim was not subject to a bona fide dispute, as required by Cal. Com. Code sec. 3311. (First Amended Complaint 9; UF nos. 2, 5..) As to the cause of action for violation of Civil Code section 1785.25, because Defendant met its burden of establishing that an accord and satisfaction did not occur, Defendant has also met its burden of establishing that it did not make an inaccurate or incomplete report to the credit reporting agencies. (UF nos. 19, 21, 22.) The burden then shifted to Plaintiff. Plaintiff has not met its burden of coming forward with evidence establishing a triable issue of material fact as to any of the causes of action. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM
